skill.name: tanstack-integration
skill.description: Evaluate React or Next.js projects for focused TanStack Query, Table, Form, Router, and Virtual adoption opportunities after the app works.
skill.summary: Identify where TanStack libraries can simplify existing React and Next.js code.
skill.author: Dicklesworthstone

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are Markdown prompts, local API examples, and prose rather than executable skill code or malicious instructions.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The backtick detections are Markdown code fences, inline code references, TypeScript examples, and bead task examples. No Ruby shell execution or executable skill script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples in SKILL.md, not code that the skill executes. There is no interpreter entry point or command invocation outside Markdown.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The network detections are a generic mention of fetch or axios and local /api/users examples. No external domain, credential transfer, or hidden request is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only fetch calls target a relative local API path inside illustrative TypeScript snippets. No external endpoint or exfiltration pattern appears in the file.

low_findings.title: Blocker Keyword Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance flags match benign prose about TanStack analysis and table components. No evidence found for cryptographic code, system probing, or prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text or user-facing review prompts. They do not contain encryption routines, host inspection commands, or instruction override language.

content.value_statement: React apps often accumulate manual fetching, table, form, and routing logic. This skill helps identify TanStack libraries that can simplify specific code paths.
actual_capabilities: Identify data fetching patterns that may benefit from TanStack Query.
Find complex table or grid components that may fit TanStack Table.
Review form, routing, and virtualization needs against TanStack options.
Encourage selective adoption after the existing React or Next.js app works.
Provide focused prompts for broad or library-specific codebase reviews.
limitations: It does not install, configure, or migrate TanStack libraries by itself.
It is most useful for React or Next.js projects with existing implementation details.
Its recommendations still need validation against bundle size and team familiarity.
Small static apps may not benefit from TanStack adoption.

use_cases.title: Review a Mature Frontend
use_cases.description: Find places where manual client state or server state logic has become hard to maintain.

use_cases.title: Plan a Focused Refactor
use_cases.description: Choose a limited TanStack migration that improves one table, form, route, or data flow.

use_cases.title: Compare Library Fit
use_cases.description: Decide whether vanilla React, Next.js features, or a TanStack library is the best fit.

prompt_templates.title: Find TanStack Opportunities
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing app
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React or Next.js project for places where TanStack could simplify existing code. Focus on clear benefits, not broad adoption.

prompt_templates.title: Review Data Fetching
prompt_templates.prompt: Look for data fetching patterns that may benefit from TanStack Query. Consider caching, refetching, pagination, optimistic updates, and loading states.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Tables and Forms
prompt_templates.prompt: Find table, grid, and form components that may benefit from TanStack Table or TanStack Form. Explain the expected maintenance benefit.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Selective Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a selective TanStack migration plan for the best candidates. Include priority, expected impact, risks, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Review a dashboard with repeated manual loading, error, and refetch state around user data.
output: TanStack Query is a strong candidate for shared server state.
Expected benefit: less duplicate state handling and more consistent refetch behavior.
Validate by checking cache keys, stale time, and mutation needs.

output_examples.input: Review an admin table with sorting, filtering, pagination, and row selection.
output: TanStack Table is a strong candidate because table behavior is already complex.
Expected benefit: centralized table state with headless UI control.
Avoid migration if the table is static or rarely used.

output_examples.input: Review a small contact form with four fields and simple validation.
output: TanStack Form is not recommended for this case.
The existing simple form likely has lower complexity and lower bundle impact.
Revisit only if dynamic fields or async validation are added.

best_practices: Start from a working vanilla implementation before considering TanStack libraries.
Adopt one TanStack library only where the current code has clear complexity.
Validate each recommendation with bundle impact, tests, and team familiarity.
anti_patterns: Installing every TanStack package before the app has real complexity.
Replacing simple static tables, forms, or fetches without measurable benefit.
Treating TanStack as a default architecture for every React feature.

faq.question: Does this skill write TanStack code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides analysis and planning. Implementation still requires normal coding, review, and testing.

faq.question: Which TanStack libraries does it consider?
faq.answer: It covers Query, Table, Form, Router, Virtual, and related TanStack options mentioned in the skill.

faq.question: Should I use it on a new project?
faq.answer: Use it after the project has working React or Next.js code. The skill discourages premature adoption.

faq.question: Can it help with Next.js App Router projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but it may recommend keeping Next.js routing when TanStack Router adds no clear benefit.

faq.question: Does it replace performance profiling?
faq.answer: No. It can identify candidates, but performance claims should be checked with measurements.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It does not add network code. Users should still follow their normal repository access policies.
